audit_items.proof_trace: remove_from_block_list calls strlen(addr) then cache_remove(block_list, addr, addr_len); it never frees addr. cache_remove (cache.c:162-186) frees only its internal heap copies: tmp->data (via free_cb or ss_free), then tmp->key (the internally-allocated key copy from cache_insert), then tmp itself — in that order, each exactly once, with no post-free access. The caller-supplied addr pointer is never freed in this call chain, making UAF and double-free impossible within this function.
